Research & Papers.title: Large Language Models over Networks: Collaborative Intelligence under Resource Constraints
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.08626v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are transforming society, powering applications from smartphone assistants to autonomous driving. Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Energy-based models (EBMs) provide a powerful and flexible way of learning a joint probability distribution over data by constructing an energy surface. Abstract: Over the last two decades, language modeling has experienced a shift from predominantly recurrent architectures that process tokens sequentially during training and inference to non-recurrent models that process sequence elements in parallel during training, which results in greater training efficiency and stability at the expense of lower inference throughput. Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: JPEG decode is routine ML infrastructure, but Python decoder choices are often justified by single-process, single-thread microbenchmarks. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.08732v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Modern vision-based world models can represent observations as compact yet expressive latent manifolds, but fast goal-oriented planning in these spaces remains challenging. Research & Papers.title: Beyond the All-in-One Agent: Benchmarking Role-Specialized Multi-Agent Collaboration in Enterprise Workflows
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.08761v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly expected to operate in enterprise environments, where work is distributed across specialized roles, permission-controlled systems, and cross-departmental procedures. Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Deep learning techniques have revolutionised medical imaging, improving diagnostic accuracy and enabling both more accurate and earlier disease detection. Abstract: Hallucination detection has become increasingly important for improving the reliability of large language models (LLMs). Research & Papers.title: Tight Generalization Bounds for Noiseless Inverse Optimization

arXiv:2605.08866v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Inverse optimization (IO) seeks to infer the parameters of a decision-maker's objective from observed context--action data. Research & Papers.summary:

Research & Papers.title: LLiMba: Sardinian on a Single GPU -- Adapting a 3B Language Model to a Vanishing Romance Language

Research & Papers.title: MedFL-Stress: A Systematic Robustness Evaluation of Federated Brain Tumor Segmentation under Cross-Hospital MRI Appearance Shift

Abstract: Federated learning enables hospitals to collaboratively train segmentation models without sharing patient data. Research & Papers.title: Privacy-Preserving Distributed Learning in IoT Systems: A Unified Threat Model and Evaluation Framework
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: The increasing deployment of Internet-of-Things (IoT) devices has accelerated the use of distributed learning frameworks, where data remains local while model updates are shared across decentralized systems. Research & Papers.title: Repeated-Token Counting Reveals a Dissociation Between Representations and Outputs
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Large language models fail at counting repeated tokens despite strong performance on broader reasoning benchmarks. Research & Papers.title: Reinforcement Learning Measurement Model

Abstract: GUI agents are beginning to operate the web, mobile, and desktop as interactive worlds, where successful control depends on carrying forward visual, procedural, and task-level evidence beyond the fleeting present screen. Research & Papers.title: Test-Time Speculation

Abstract: Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference by using a fast draft model to generate tokens and a more accurate target model to verify them. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.09342v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Autonomous drone fleets have immense potential in medical supply delivery during disaster incident response. However, coordinating multiple drones in such settings introduces compounding challenges: dynamic environmental hazards such as wind, obstacles, and intermittent network connectivity, constrained energy budgets, and the need to serve patient locations fairly under deadlines and triage-based priority while optimizing schedule utilization.

Abstract: Running deep neural networks on microcontroller units (MCUs) is severely constrained by limited memory resources. Abstract: Reasoning LLMs produce thousands of chain-of-thought tokens whose KV cache must reside in scarce GPU HBM. Research & Papers.title: DRIFT: Drift-Resilient Invariant-Feature Transformer for DGA Detection

Research & Papers.title: SpecBlock: Block-Iterative Speculative Decoding with Dynamic Tree Drafting

Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.07248v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Beyond training-time optimization, scaling test-time computation has emerged as a key paradigm to extend the reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). Abstract: Multiple-choice reasoning benchmarks face dual challenges: rapid saturation from advancing models and data contamination that undermines static evaluations. Abstract: Layer pruning efficiently reduces Large Language Model (LLM) computational costs but often triggers sudden performance collapse. Abstract: Natural Language Processing is rapidly evolving into a primary instrument for Computational Social Science, with researchers increasingly using embeddings to measure latent constructs such as novelty, creativity, and bias. Abstract: Instruction following is a fundamental capability of large language models (LLMs), yet continuously improving this capability remains challenging. Research & Papers.title: Post-training makes large language models less human-like
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.07632v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as surrogates for human participants, but it remains unclear which models best capture human behavior and why. We present CMR-EXTR, a lightweight framework that converts free-text CMR reports into structured data and assigns per-field confidence for quality control. A teacher-student distillation pipeline enables fully offline inference while limiting manual annotation.

Research & Papers.title: CA-SQL: Complexity-Aware Inference Time Reasoning for Text-to-SQL via Exploration and Compute Budget Allocation
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.08057v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: While recent advancements in inference-time learning have improved LLM reasoning on Text-to-SQL tasks, current solutions still struggle to perform well on the most challenging tasks in the Bird-Bench (BIRD) benchmark. Abstract: Context window expansion is often treated as a straightforward capability upgrade for LLMs, but we find it systematically fails in multi-agent social dilemmas. Research & Papers.title: Conformal Path Reasoning: Trustworthy Knowledge Graph Question Answering via Path-Level Calibration
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Knowledge Graph Question Answering (KGQA) has shown promise for grounded and interpretable reasoning, yet existing approaches often fail to provide reliable coverage guarantees over retrieved answers. Research & Papers.title: LLMs Improving LLMs: Agentic Discovery for Test-Time Scaling
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.08083v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Test-time scaling (TTS) has become an effective approach for improving large language model performance by allocating additional computation during inference. Research & Papers.title: More Thinking, More Bias: Length-Driven Position Bias in Reasoning Models

Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning and reasoning-tuned models such as DeepSeek-R1 are commonly assumed to reduce shallow heuristic biases by thinking carefully. Research & Papers.title: RateQuant: Optimal Mixed-Precision KV Cache Quantization via Rate-Distortion Theory

arXiv:2605.06675v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models cache all previously computed key-value (KV) pairs during generation, and this KV cache grows linearly with sequence length, making it a primary memory bottleneck for serving. Research & Papers.title: LKV: End-to-End Learning of Head-wise Budgets and Token Selection for LLM KV Cache Eviction

Abstract: Long-context inference in Large Language Models (LLMs) is bottlenecked by the linear growth of Key-Value (KV) cache memory. Research & Papers.title: Toeplitz MLP Mixers are Low Complexity, Information-Rich Sequence Models

Research & Papers.title: State Representation and Termination for Recursive Reasoning Systems
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.06690v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Recursive reasoning systems alternate between acquiring new evidence and refining an accumulated understanding. Research & Papers.title: CASCADE: Case-Based Continual Adaptation for Large Language Models During Deployment
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.06702v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become a central foundation of modern artificial intelligence, yet their lifecycle remains constrained by a rigid separation between training and deployment, after which learning effectively ceases. Research & Papers.title: When Does a Language Model Commit? A Finite-Answer Theory of Pre-Verbalization Commitment
Research & Papers.summary:

arXiv:2605.06731v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Personalized LLM agents maintain persistent cross-session state to support long-horizon collaboration. Research & Papers.title: ProtSent: Protein Sentence Transformers

Abstract: Recent methods expose intra-request parallelism in LLM outputs, allowing independent branches to decode concurrently. Abstract: Architectural code smells erode software maintainability and are costly to repair manually, yet unlike localized bugs, they require cross-module reasoning about design intent that challenges both developers and automated tools. Research & Papers.title: Theoretical Limits of Language Model Alignment

Abstract: Language model (LM) alignment improves model outputs to reflect human preferences while preserving the capabilities of the base model. Abstract: Modern large language models (LLMs) can find a needle in a haystack (locating a single relevant fact buried among hundreds of thousands of irrelevant tokens) with near-saturated accuracy, yet fail to retrieve the last few items in a short list. Research & Papers.title: Topic Is Not Agenda: A Citation-Community Audit of Text Embeddings
Research & Papers.summary:

Research & Papers.title: MEMOREPAIR: Barrier-First Cascade Repair in Agentic Memory

Research & Papers.title: Experience Sharing in Mutual Reinforcement Learning for Heterogeneous Language Models

Research & Papers.title: When Are Experts Misrouted? Counterfactual Routing Analysis in Mixture-of-Experts Language Models

Abstract: Efficient routing across multiple LLMs enables cost-quality tradeoffs by directing queries to the cheapest capable model. Research & Papers.summary:

Research & Papers.title: Sparse Autoencoders as Plug-and-Play Firewalls for Adversarial Attack Detection in VLMs
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.07447v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Vision-language models (VLMs) have advanced rapidly and are increasingly deployed in real-world applications, especially with the rise of agent-based systems. Research & Papers.summary:

Research & Papers.summary:

Research & Papers.title: KL for a KL: On-Policy Distillation with Control Variate Baseline

Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2603.03096v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: How do speech models trained through self-supervised learning structure their representations? Previous studies have looked at how information is encoded in feature vectors across different layers. But few studies have considered whether speech characteristics are captured within individual dimensions of SSL features. In this paper we specifically look at speaker information using PCA on utterance-averaged representations. For a range of

Research & Papers.title: Sparser, Faster, Lighter Transformer Language Models
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2603.23198v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: Scaling autoregressive large language models (LLMs) has driven unprecedented progress but comes with vast computational costs. In this work, we tackle these costs by leveraging unstructured sparsity within an LLM's feedforward layers, the components accounting for most of the model parameters and execution FLOPs. To achieve this, we introduce a new sparse packing format and a set of CUDA kernels designed to seamlessly integrate with the

Research & Papers.title: SlopCodeBench: Benchmarking How Coding Agents Degrade Over Long-Horizon Iterative Tasks
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2603.24755v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: Software development is iterative, yet agentic coding benchmarks hide design issues through their single-shot setup. Recent iterative benchmarks attempt to remedy this but heavily constrain an agent's design decision space, making it impossible to faithfully measure how their decisions shape future extensions. We introduce SlopCodeBench, a benchmark of 36 problems and 196 checkpoints where agents repeatedly extend their own solutions. Un

Research & Papers.title: OASES: Outcome-Aligned Search-Evaluation Co-Training for Agentic Search
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.03675v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: Agentic search enables language models to solve knowledge-intensive tasks by adaptively acquiring external evidence over multiple steps. Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a widely adopted training paradigm for search agents, yet outcome-only rewards are sparse and provide limited credit assignment for intermediate search actions. Existing process-reward methods therefore seek to densify supervision thro

Research & Papers.title: KV Cache Offloading for Context-Intensive Tasks
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2604.08426v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: With the growing demand for long-context LLMs across a wide range of applications, the key-value (KV) cache has become a critical bottleneck for both latency and memory usage. Recently, KV-cache offloading has emerged as a promising approach to reduce memory footprint and inference latency while preserving accuracy. Prior evaluations have largely focused on tasks that do not require extracting large amounts of information from the contex

Research & Papers.title: ResRL: Boosting LLM Reasoning via Negative Sample Projection Residual Reinforcement Learning
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.00380v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) enhances reasoning of Large Language Models (LLMs) but usually exhibits limited generation diversity due to the over-incentivization of positive rewards. Although methods like Negative Sample Reinforcement (NSR) mitigate this issue by upweighting penalty from negative samples, they may suppress the semantic distributions shared between positive and negative responses. To boost reasoni

Research & Papers.title: Multilingual Safety Alignment via Self-Distillation
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.02971v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit severe multilingual safety misalignment: they possess strong safeguards in high-resource languages but remain highly vulnerable to jailbreak attacks in low-resource languages. Current safety alignment methods generally rely on high-quality response data for each target language, which is expensive and difficult to generate. In this paper, we propose a cross-lingual safeguard transfer framework named M

Research & Papers.title: FAAST: Forward-Only Associative Learning via Closed-Form Fast Weights for Test-Time Supervised Adaptation
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04651v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: Adapting pretrained models typically involves a trade-off between the high training costs of backpropagation and the heavy inference overhead of memory-based or in-context learning. We propose FAAST, a forward-only associative adaptation method that analytically compiles labeled examples into fast weights in a single pass. Research & Papers.title: Belief Memory: Agent Memory Under Partial Observability
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.05583v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: LLM agents that operate over long context depend on external memory to accumulate knowledge over time. However, existing methods typically store each observation as a single deterministic conclusion (e.g., inferring "API~X failed" from temporary errors), even though such observations are inherently partial and potentially ambiguous. By committing to one conclusion and discarding uncertainty, these methods introduce self-reinforcing error

Research & Papers.title: Safety Anchor: Defending Harmful Fine-tuning via Geometric Bottlenecks
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.05995v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: The safety alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) remains vulnerable to Harmful Fine-tuning (HFT). While existing defenses impose constraints on parameters, gradients, or internal representations, we observe that they can be effectively circumvented under persistent HFT. Our analysis traces this failure to the inherent redundancy of the high-dimensional parameter space: attackers exploit optimization trajectories that are orthogonal t

Research & Papers.title: On Time, Within Budget: Constraint-Driven Online Resource Allocation for Agentic Workflows
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.06110v2 Announce Type: replace-cross 
Abstract: Agentic systems increasingly solve complex user requests by executing orchestrated workflows, where subtasks are assigned to specialized models or tools and coordinated according to their dependencies. While recent work improves agent efficiency by optimizing the performance--cost--latency frontier, real deployments often impose concrete requirements: a workflow must be completed within a specified budget and before a specified deadline.

Fund News & Trends.title: China Dominates Offshore Wind as Global Tensions Rise

From the South China Sea, Bloomberg’s Stephen Engle reports on Ming Yang’s new 50-megawatt floating turbine and how 45% lower pricing is securing China’s lead in the global clean-energy race. (Source: Bloomberg)

Fund News & Trends.title: Australia Tackles Property Tax Breaks in Budget
Fund News & Trends.summary: Australia will overhaul tax settings that have long favored property investors and assist households and firms grappling with soaring fuel costs in a budget presented by Treasurer Jim Chalmers. (Source: Bloomberg)

Fund News & Trends.title: Hedge Fund CFM Opens Office in Shanghai, Assets Top $27 Billion
Fund News & Trends.summary: French quantitative hedge fund Capital Fund Management has opened an office in Shanghai, following in the footsteps of peers in establishing a presence in the world’s second-largest economy.

Fund News & Trends.title: TIAA's Kourtney Gibson: Us Economy Showing Some ‘Wear'
Fund News & Trends.summary: TIAA CEO, Retirement Solutions Kourtney Gibson discusses retirement in a complex market landscape. She speaks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on "The Close."  (Source: Bloomberg)

Fund News & Trends.title: Goldman Sees Dollar Strength as Energy Shock to Keep Rates High
Fund News & Trends.summary: The dollar strength will build further over the near term as the energy-price shock will keep yields elevated as economic growth remains relatively resilient, according to Goldman Sachs Group.

Fund News & Trends.title: Market 'Yet to Fully Experience' Aluminum Shortfall from Iran, Says Timna Tanners
Fund News & Trends.summary: Shortfalls could persist longer than current expectations and drive up the price of aluminum as an impact of war with Iran says Timna Tanners, managing director of equity research for Wells Fargo. Tanners talked about the different impacts of the war on commodity prices, including copper which neared record highs on Tuesday due to a rise in demand from China and fears about supplies of sulfur in the Mideast.  (Source: Bloomberg)

Fund News & Trends.title: Ananym Capital Urges BWX to Make Commercial Nuclear Reactors
Fund News & Trends.summary: Activist fund Ananym Capital Management is pushing nuclear technology company BWX Technologies Inc. to tap its nuclear reactor manufacturing capabilities for commercial use.

Fund News & Trends.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras to Guide IPO Pricing Above Range
Fund News & Trends.summary: Cerebras Systems Inc. is guiding prospective investors that it expects to price its initial public offering above the top of its marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand soars for the chipmaker’s stock.

Fund News & Trends.title: Mexico Rating Outlook Revised to Negative at S&P as Debts Mount
Fund News & Trends.summary: S&P Global Ratings revised Mexico’s credit outlook to negative from stable, citing persistently weak fiscal results, rising debt levels and weak economic growth.

Fund News & Trends.title: Trump's Goal In China? Keep It Simple
Fund News & Trends.summary: Expectations are running high for the US-China summit in Beijing, but Trump’s main goal should be to not make things worse, the Editorial Board writes. (Source: Bloomberg)

Fund News & Trends.title: Brazil Meat Left Out of EU Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
Fund News & Trends.summary: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef and chicken exporter.

Platforms & Products.title: Eagle Bancorp hires Western Alliance executive as next CEO
Platforms & Products.summary: Stephen Curley, who was the chief banking officer at Phoenix-based Western Alliance, will join Maryland-based Eagle Bancorp as president and CEO in July. Eagle's current CEO previously announced plans to retire after the bank racked up big losses in office loans.

Platforms & Products.title: Amid credit snags, Western Alliance defends its lending practices
Platforms & Products.summary: At its first-ever investor day, the Phoenix-based bank fended off questions stemming from two recently disclosed problem loans, including one that resulted in a $126 million charge-off.

Platforms & Products.title: Late student-loan payments are rising. Is a domino effect coming?
Platforms & Products.summary: The 90-day-plus delinquency rate on student loans hit 10.3% in the first quarter, and New York Fed researchers warn that a second wave of defaults could be coming. Evidence is mixed regarding the likely impact on other consumer-lending segments.

Platforms & Products.title: Warsh confirmed to Fed board
Platforms & Products.summary: Kevin Warsh's nomination to the Federal Reserve chairmanship will require a second vote, which should happen this week.

Platforms & Products.title: ICBA urges OCC to halt Kraken parent's trust charter bid
Platforms & Products.summary: The community bank trade group says crypto firms are combining stablecoin rules, Fed master account access and trust charters to replicate banking without bank rules.

Platforms & Products.title: Why BMO is selling its transportation, vendor finance portfolios
Platforms & Products.summary: The Canadian bank signed an agreement to sell the businesses to Stonepeak, an alternative investment firm in New York. The move will free up capital and allow the bank to invest in higher growth-potential areas.

Platforms & Products.title: Treasury staffer Crews nominated to lead NCUA
Platforms & Products.summary: President Donald Trump has tapped Treasury official John Crews to be the next chair of the National Credit Union Administration.

Platforms & Products.title: Corporate card fintech Parker shuts down without warning
Platforms & Products.summary: Parker Group unexpectedly ceased operating last week, then filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy a few days later as sponsor banks and customers were left scrambling.

Platforms & Products.title: Energy shock drives consumer prices up 0.6% in April
Platforms & Products.summary: The Consumer Price Index for April showed consumer prices rising across all categories, including food and shelter costs, and up 3.8% year-over-year. Grocery prices rose 0.7% from the month prior.

Platforms & Products.title: Why advisors should see life settlements as a planning option
Platforms & Products.summary: Allowing clients to surrender or lapse life insurance policies before gauging value on the secondary market amounts to a fiduciary fail, argues Cole Hallman of Citizens Life Group.
